items.title: Indigenous veteran booed at Anzac Day service 'mortified' by heckling

children.content: The Whadjuk Noongar elder who was booed while giving a Welcome to Country at Perth's Anzac Day dawn service says she had a right to speak at the event and will not back down.

articles.title: Leaders condemn 'disgusting' disruptions by 'cretins' at WA Anzac Day services

children.content: Leaders have condemned "cretins" who interjected and booed at Perth and Western Australian Anzac Day ceremonies, with the premier labelling their actions "disgusting and disrespectful".

image.altText: The silhouette of a soldier at the 2026 Anzac Day dawn service in Geraldton.

articles.title: Veteran's advice after Anzac Day booing: Find your 'warrior spirit'

children.content: While condemnation has been swift after Indigenous elders were booed on Anzac Day, solutions to this type of racism seem harder to pin down. Elders have advice to share.

image.altText: uncle ray at anzac day service at redfern

items.title: 'We live up here to escape': Residents unhappy with sand dredging proposal

children.content: A Dutch dredging company acknowledges concerns from some community members in northern Western Australia that public meetings should have been held earlier.

image.altText: A man sits next to some reeds near a river

items.title: Two young girls die as WA records another dire weekend on roads

children.content: Two young girls, aged three and four, die more than a week after a car crash in Viveash, with the news emerging after another two fatalities on WA roads over the Anzac Day long weekend.

image.altText: A section of road with some trees alongside.

items.title: Supreme Court Justice says new act 'impossible' to fully implement

children.content: WA legal experts say the Criminal Law Mental Impairment Act isn't working as intended, citing a case in Broome where a man was found not criminally responsible but still jailed.

items.title: SA Police search waterways for knives linked to alleged murder by Perth man

children.content: SA Police will search waterways in Adelaide's south today to find three knives they believe are connected to the alleged murder of 24-year-old Caleb Leriano.

image.altText: A young man with glasse smiles at the camera in a bright room

items.title: Green on form as Knight Riders win IPL thriller

children.content: It's taken a while but Cameron Green is showing why he's worth the big pay packet as he helped Kolkata win an astonishing IPL Super Over affair against Lucknow.

image.altText: Cameron Green plays a pull shot

items.title: Aussie wins first World Surf League title at Margaret River Pro

children.content: Australian surfer George Pittar defeated a quartet of Brazilian superstars — including three-time world champion Gabriel Medina in the final — to claim a maiden championship tour win at the Margaret River Pro.

items.title: One industry rules the roost in WA and neither side of politics can ignore it

children.content: Last week marked the first time the federal Liberal leader visited WA since he took the position, and during his trip west, both Angus Taylor and Premier Roger Cook were navigating the state's hot-button issues.

items.title: ASX 200 down, oil prices rising, AUD stronger

children.content: The ASX 200 has made a disappointing start to the week, despite both the S&P 500 and tech-centric Nasdaq indexes setting new record highs, while oil prices are surging with Brent crude around $US107 a barrel. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A stock trader looks down at a tablet as he works on the floor of a busy stock exchange while surrounded by screens.

items.title: Australia's fugitives found sanctuary in Istanbul. Then it all fell apart

children.content: Istanbul was once the hideout of choice for Australian crooks. Now a Sydney gangster's legal challenge has revealed how it all unravelled for the "Aussie Cartel".

image.altText: A graphic of a bearded man in grainy black-and-white against a red backdrop of the Turkish flag and Istanbul skyline.

items.title: Mother's anguish after daughter dies in prison cell

children.content: Deaths in custody are on the rise and family members and advocates for a woman who died in a Perth prison say more needs to be done to keep inmates safe.

thumbnail.altText: A woman sits at dusk holding a candle.

items.title: Frustration with war effort sparks renewed push to bring down Netanyahu — as it happened

children.content: Former Israeli prime ministers Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid are merging their two parties into a new entity called "Together" in a bid to defeat Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the next election.

image.altText: Two smiling Israeli men in suits wave to supporters as they stand in front of a blue and white background.

items.title: Sabastian Sawe ran the marathon in an 'unbelievable time'. Here's why we can believe in it

children.content: Sabastian Sawe smashes one of athletics' seemingly unbreakable barriers by running a marathon faster than two hours. Some call it unbelievable, but the Kenyan runner has done everything he can to ensure it stands the test of time.

image.altText: Sabastian Sawe stands next to his record time

items.title: Dingo destroyed after week of attacks in WA's north-west

children.content: A male dingo has been "humanely destroyed" after multiple attacks within days of each other at Western Australia's famous Karijini National Park.

items.title: 'Membership to nothing': The multi-million-dollar Wild West of rewards clubs

children.content: "Rewards clubs" have exploded in Australia, claiming to have given away hundreds of millions in prizes, but they operate with almost no legal scrutiny.

image.altText: A large 4 wheel drive in a showroom clouded in smoke with dramatic red lighting.

items.title: An oil shock, a federal budget and the recession we may have to have

children.content: Accompanying the oil shock unfolding in the Strait of Hormuz is a profound change in the way fiscal and monetary policymakers operate. And as the government prepares the budget with the impact of the Iran war still unfolding, a recession may be ahead.

image.altText: A man in a suit from a side profile with his chin jutted out defiantly.

items.title: Why the bedroom is an 'invisible workplace' for women

children.content: There are biological and psychological reasons behind why women experience more disrupted sleep than men, but experts say the mental load is another powerful yet less obvious contributor.

items.title: Trump turmoil is political kryptonite in Australia

items.title: US allies turning to Japan for defence supplies as Iran war drags on

children.content: Australia's multi-billion-dollar deal with Japan for its future warships could set a template that will help Tokyo rapidly expand defence exports across Asia.

items.title: Dawson fights back tears on return after Saints thrash Eagles by 101 points
